title: Maximum Element in Array
description: Find the maximum value in an array of integers. The array may contain negative numbers.

templates.python: def solve(arr): return max(arr)
templates.javascript: function solve(arr){return Math.max(...arr);}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<int>& arr){return *max_element(arr.begin(), arr.end());}

title: Check if Array is Sorted
description: Determine if the array is non-decreasing.

templates.python: def solve(arr): return all(arr[i]<=arr[i+1] for i in range(len(arr)-1))
templates.javascript: function solve(arr){for(let i=0;i<arr.length-1;i++)if(arr[i]>arr[i+1])return false;return true;}
templates.cpp: bool solve(vector<int>& arr){for(int i=0;i+1<arr.size();i++)if(arr[i]>arr[i+1])return false;return true;}

title: Reverse a String
description: Reverse the given string in-place.

templates.python: def solve(s): return s[::-1]
templates.javascript: function solve(s){return s.split('').reverse().join('');}
templates.cpp: string solve(string s){reverse(s.begin(),s.end());return s;}

title: Valid Palindrome
description: Check if a string is a palindrome after ignoring case and non-alphanumeric.

examples.input: A man a plan a canal Panama

templates.python: def solve(s): cleaned=''.join(c.lower() for c in s if c.isalnum()); return cleaned==cleaned[::-1]
templates.javascript: function solve(s){let c=s.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g,'');return c===c.split('').reverse().join('');}
templates.cpp: bool solve(string s){string clean;for(char c:s)if(isalnum(c))clean+=tolower(c);string rev=clean;reverse(rev.begin(),rev.end());return clean==rev;}

title: Factorial

templates.python: def solve(n): res=1; for i in range(2,n+1): res*=i; return res
templates.javascript: function solve(n){let r=1n;for(let i=2;i<=n;i++)r*=BigInt(i);return Number(r);}
templates.cpp: long long solve(int n){long long r=1;for(int i=2;i<=n;i++)r*=i;return r;}

title: Fibonacci (Recursive)
description: Compute Fibonacci number F(n) using recursion.

templates.python: def solve(n): return n if n<=1 else solve(n-1)+solve(n-2)
templates.javascript: function solve(n){return n<=1?n:solve(n-1)+solve(n-2);}
templates.cpp: int solve(int n){return n<=1?n:solve(n-1)+solve(n-2);}

title: Bubble Sort
description: Sort array using bubble sort.

templates.python: def solve(arr): n=len(arr); for i in range(n): for j in range(0,n-i-1): if arr[j]>arr[j+1]: arr[j],arr[j+1]=arr[j+1],arr[j]; return arr
templates.javascript: function solve(arr){let n=arr.length;for(let i=0;i<n;i++)for(let j=0;j<n-i-1;j++)if(arr[j]>arr[j+1])[arr[j],arr[j+1]]=[arr[j+1],arr[j]];return arr;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& arr){int n=arr.size();for(int i=0;i<n;i++)for(int j=0;j<n-i-1;j++)if(arr[j]>arr[j+1])swap(arr[j],arr[j+1]);return arr;}

title: Linear Search

title: Reverse Linked List

templates.python:
class ListNode: def __init__(self,val=0,next=None): self.val=val; self.next=next
def solve(head): prev=None; curr=head; while curr: nxt=curr.next; curr.next=prev; prev=curr; curr=nxt; return prev
templates.javascript: function solve(head){let prev=null,curr=head;while(curr){let nxt=curr.next;curr.next=prev;prev=curr;curr=nxt;}return prev;}

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Check if brackets are correctly matched.

templates.python: def solve(s): stack=[]; pairs={')':'(',']':'[','}':'{'}; for ch in s: if ch in pairs: if not stack or stack.pop()!=pairs[ch]: return False; else: stack.append(ch); return not stack
templates.javascript: function solve(s){let st=[];let map={')':'(',']':'[','}':'{'};for(let ch of s){if(map[ch]){if(st.length===0||st.pop()!==map[ch])return false;}else st.push(ch);}return st.length===0;}
templates.cpp: bool solve(string s){stack<char> st; unordered_map<char,char> mp={{')','('},{']','['},{'}','{'}}; for(char c:s){if(mp.count(c)){if(st.empty()||st.top()!=mp[c])return false; st.pop();}else st.push(c);}return st.empty();}

title: Implement Queue using Stacks
description: Implement a FIFO queue using two stacks.

templates.python: class MyQueue: def __init__(self): self.s1=[]; self.s2=[]; def push(self,x): self.s1.append(x); def pop(self): if not self.s2: while self.s1: self.s2.append(self.s1.pop()); return self.s2.pop(); def peek(self): if not self.s2: while self.s1: self.s2.append(self.s1.pop()); return self.s2[-1]; def empty(self): return not self.s1 and not self.s2
templates.javascript: class MyQueue{constructor(){this.s1=[];this.s2=[];}push(x){this.s1.push(x);}pop(){if(this.s2.length===0){while(this.s1.length)this.s2.push(this.s1.pop());}return this.s2.pop();}peek(){if(this.s2.length===0){while(this.s1.length)this.s2.push(this.s1.pop());}return this.s2[this.s2.length-1];}empty(){return this.s1.length===0&&this.s2.length===0;}}
templates.cpp: class MyQueue{stack<int> s1,s2; public: void push(int x){s1.push(x);} int pop(){if(s2.empty()){while(!s1.empty()){s2.push(s1.top());s1.pop();}}int x=s2.top();s2.pop();return x;} int peek(){if(s2.empty()){while(!s1.empty()){s2.push(s1.top());s1.pop();}}return s2.top();} bool empty(){return s1.empty()&&s2.empty();}};

hints: Use two stacks: one for input, one for output.

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Return max in each sliding window of size k.

templates.python:
from collections import deque
def solve(arr,k): dq=deque(); res=[]; for i,x in enumerate(arr): if dq and dq[0]<=i-k: dq.popleft(); while dq and arr[dq[-1]]<=x: dq.pop(); dq.append(i); if i>=k-1: res.append(arr[dq[0]]); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,k){let dq=[],res=[];for(let i=0;i<arr.length;i++){if(dq.length&&dq[0]<=i-k)dq.shift();while(dq.length&&arr[dq[dq.length-1]]<=arr[i])dq.pop();dq.push(i);if(i>=k-1)res.push(arr[dq[0]]);}return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& arr,int k){deque<int> dq; vector<int> res; for(int i=0;i<arr.size();i++){if(!dq.empty()&&dq.front()<=i-k)dq.pop_front();while(!dq.empty()&&arr[dq.back()]<=arr[i])dq.pop_back();dq.push_back(i);if(i>=k-1)res.push_back(arr[dq.front()]);}return res;}

hints: Use deque to maintain decreasing order.

title: Two Sum
description: Check if any two numbers sum to target.

templates.python: def solve(arr,target): seen=set(); for x in arr: if target-x in seen: return True; seen.add(x); return False
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,target){let seen=new Set();for(let x of arr){if(seen.has(target-x))return true;seen.add(x);}return false;}
templates.cpp: bool solve(vector<int>& arr,int target){unordered_set<int> seen; for(int x:arr){if(seen.count(target-x))return true; seen.insert(x);} return false;}

title: First Unique Character
description: Find first non-repeating character in a string.

templates.python:
from collections import Counter
def solve(s): count=Counter(s); for i,ch in enumerate(s): if count[ch]==1: return i; return -1
templates.javascript: function solve(s){let map={};for(let ch of s)map[ch]=(map[ch]||0)+1;for(let i=0;i<s.length;i++)if(map[s[i]]===1)return i;return -1;}
templates.cpp: int solve(string s){unordered_map<char,int> cnt; for(char c:s)cnt[c]++; for(int i=0;i<s.size();i++)if(cnt[s[i]]==1)return i; return -1;}

title: Intersection of Two Arrays
description: Return common elements between two arrays.

templates.python: def solve(arr1,arr2): return list(set(arr1)&set(arr2))
templates.javascript: function solve(arr1,arr2){return arr1.filter(x=>arr2.includes(x));}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& a,vector<int>& b){unordered_set<int> s(a.begin(),a.end()); vector<int> res; for(int x:b)if(s.count(x)){res.push_back(x);s.erase(x);}return res;}

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
description: Remove duplicates in-place and return new length.

templates.python: def solve(arr): pos=1; for i in range(1,len(arr)): if arr[i]!=arr[i-1]: arr[pos]=arr[i]; pos+=1; return pos, arr[:pos]
templates.javascript: function solve(arr){let pos=1;for(let i=1;i<arr.length;i++){if(arr[i]!==arr[i-1]){arr[pos]=arr[i];pos++;}}return [pos,arr.slice(0,pos)];}
templates.cpp: pair<int,vector<int>> solve(vector<int>& arr){int pos=1;for(int i=1;i<arr.size();i++){if(arr[i]!=arr[i-1])arr[pos++]=arr[i];}return {pos, vector<int>(arr.begin(),arr.begin()+pos)};}

title: Two Sum II (Sorted)
description: Find two numbers in sorted array that sum to target.

templates.python: def solve(arr,target): l=0; r=len(arr)-1; while l<r: s=arr[l]+arr[r]; if s==target: return [l+1,r+1]; elif s<target: l+=1; else: r-=1; return []
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,target){let l=0,r=arr.length-1;while(l<r){let s=arr[l]+arr[r];if(s===target)return [l+1,r+1];else if(s<target)l++;else r--;}return [];}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& arr,int target){int l=0,r=arr.size()-1; while(l<r){int s=arr[l]+arr[r]; if(s==target)return {l+1,r+1}; else if(s<target)l++; else r--;} return {};}

title: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
description: Find maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of length k.

templates.python: def solve(arr,k): curr=sum(arr[:k]); max_sum=curr; for i in range(k,len(arr)): curr+=arr[i]-arr[i-k]; max_sum=max(max_sum,curr); return max_sum
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,k){let curr=0;for(let i=0;i<k;i++)curr+=arr[i];let max=curr;for(let i=k;i<arr.length;i++){curr+=arr[i]-arr[i-k];max=Math.max(max,curr);}return max;}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<int>& arr,int k){int curr=0;for(int i=0;i<k;i++)curr+=arr[i];int max_sum=curr;for(int i=k;i<arr.size();i++){curr+=arr[i]-arr[i-k];max_sum=max(max_sum,curr);}return max_sum;}

title: Range Sum Query
description: Given array, answer sum queries [l,r] using prefix sum.

templates.python: def solve(arr,queries): pref=[0]; for x in arr: pref.append(pref[-1]+x); return [pref[r+1]-pref[l] for l,r in queries]
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,queries){let pref=[0];for(let x of arr)pref.push(pref[pref.length-1]+x);return queries.map(([l,r])=>pref[r+1]-pref[l]);}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& arr, vector<pair<int,int>>& queries){vector<int> pref(arr.size()+1,0); for(int i=0;i<arr.size();i++)pref[i+1]=pref[i]+arr[i]; vector<int> ans; for(auto [l,r]:queries)ans.push_back(pref[r+1]-pref[l]); return ans;}

title: Range Updates
description: Perform range increment updates and final array.

templates.python: def solve(n, updates): diff=[0]*(n+1); for l,r,val in updates: diff[l]+=val; diff[r+1]-=val; arr=[0]*n; curr=0; for i in range(n): curr+=diff[i]; arr[i]=curr; return arr
templates.javascript: function solve(n, updates){let diff=new Array(n+1).fill(0);for(let [l,r,val] of updates){diff[l]+=val;diff[r+1]-=val;}let arr=[],curr=0;for(let i=0;i<n;i++){curr+=diff[i];arr.push(curr);}return arr;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(int n, vector<tuple<int,int,int>>& updates){vector<int> diff(n+1,0); for(auto [l,r,val]:updates){diff[l]+=val; diff[r+1]-=val;} vector<int> arr(n); int curr=0; for(int i=0;i<n;i++){curr+=diff[i]; arr[i]=curr;} return arr;}

title: Binary Search
description: Search target in sorted array, return index or -1.

templates.python: def solve(arr,target): l=0; r=len(arr)-1; while l<=r: mid=(l+r)//2; if arr[mid]==target: return mid; elif arr[mid]<target: l=mid+1; else: r=mid-1; return -1
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,target){let l=0,r=arr.length-1;while(l<=r){let mid=Math.floor((l+r)/2);if(arr[mid]===target)return mid;else if(arr[mid]<target)l=mid+1;else r=mid-1;}return -1;}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<int>& arr,int target){int l=0,r=arr.size()-1; while(l<=r){int mid=l+(r-l)/2; if(arr[mid]==target)return mid; else if(arr[mid]<target)l=mid+1; else r=mid-1;} return -1;}

title: Inorder Traversal
description: Return inorder traversal of binary tree.

templates.python: def inorder(root): res=[]; def dfs(node): if not node: return; dfs(node.left); res.append(node.val); dfs(node.right); dfs(root); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(root){let res=[];function dfs(node){if(!node)return;dfs(node.left);res.push(node.val);dfs(node.right);}dfs(root);return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(TreeNode* root){vector<int> res; function<void(TreeNode*)> dfs=[&](TreeNode* node){if(!node)return; dfs(node->left); res.push_back(node->val); dfs(node->right);}; dfs(root); return res;}

title: Maximum Depth
description: Find maximum depth of binary tree.

templates.python: def solve(root): if not root: return 0; return 1+max(solve(root.left), solve(root.right))
templates.javascript: function solve(root){return root?1+Math.max(solve(root.left),solve(root.right)):0;}
templates.cpp: int solve(TreeNode* root){return root?1+max(solve(root->left),solve(root->right)):0;}

title: Validate BST
description: Check if binary tree is a valid BST.

templates.python: def solve(root): def helper(node,low,high): if not node: return True; if not (low<node.val<high): return False; return helper(node.left,low,node.val) and helper(node.right,node.val,high); return helper(root,float('-inf'),float('inf'))
templates.javascript: function solve(root){function dfs(node,low,high){if(!node)return true;if(node.val<=low||node.val>=high)return false;return dfs(node.left,low,node.val)&&dfs(node.right,node.val,high);}return dfs(root,-Infinity,Infinity);}
templates.cpp: bool solve(TreeNode* root){function<bool(TreeNode*,long long,long long)> dfs=[&](TreeNode* node,long long low,long long high){if(!node)return true;if(node->val<=low||node->val>=high)return false;return dfs(node->left,low,node->val)&&dfs(node->right,node->val,high);}; return dfs(root,LLONG_MIN,LLONG_MAX);}

def solve(arr,k): return heapq.nlargest(k,arr)[-1]

title: Insert and Search in Trie
description: Implement insert and search in a trie.

templates.python:
class TrieNode: def __init__(self): self.children={}; self.isEnd=False
class Trie: def __init__(self): self.root=TrieNode(); def insert(self,word): node=self.root; for ch in word: if ch not in node.children: node.children[ch]=TrieNode(); node=node.children[ch]; node.isEnd=True; def search(self,word): node=self.root; for ch in word: if ch not in node.children: return False; node=node.children[ch]; return node.isEnd
templates.javascript:
class TrieNode{constructor(){this.children={};this.isEnd=false;}}
class Trie{constructor(){this.root=new TrieNode();} insert(word){let node=this.root;for(let ch of word){if(!node.children[ch])node.children[ch]=new TrieNode();node=node.children[ch];}node.isEnd=true;} search(word){let node=this.root;for(let ch of word){if(!node.children[ch])return false;node=node.children[ch];}return node.isEnd;}}
templates.cpp:
struct TrieNode{unordered_map<char,TrieNode*> children; bool isEnd=false;};
class Trie{ TrieNode* root; public: Trie(){root=new TrieNode();} void insert(string word){TrieNode* node=root; for(char c:word){if(!node->children[c])node->children[c]=new TrieNode(); node=node->children[c];} node->isEnd=true;} bool search(string word){TrieNode* node=root; for(char c:word){if(!node->children[c])return false; node=node->children[c];} return node->isEnd;}};

title: Segment Tree Sum Query
description: Build segment tree for sum and answer range sum queries.

templates.python: class SegTree: def __init__(self,arr): self.n=len(arr); self.tree=[0]*(4*self.n); self._build(arr,0,0,self.n-1); def _build(self,arr,node,l,r): if l==r: self.tree[node]=arr[l]; return; mid=(l+r)//2; self._build(arr,2*node+1,l,mid); self._build(arr,2*node+2,mid+1,r); self.tree[node]=self.tree[2*node+1]+self.tree[2*node+2]; def query(self,L,R): return self._query(0,0,self.n-1,L,R); def _query(self,node,l,r,L,R): if L<=l and r<=R: return self.tree[node]; if R<l or r<L: return 0; mid=(l+r)//2; return self._query(2*node+1,l,mid,L,R)+self._query(2*node+2,mid+1,r,L,R)
templates.javascript: class SegTree{constructor(arr){this.n=arr.length;this.tree=new Array(4*this.n);this._build(arr,0,0,this.n-1);} _build(arr,node,l,r){if(l===r){this.tree[node]=arr[l];return;}let mid=Math.floor((l+r)/2);this._build(arr,2*node+1,l,mid);this._build(arr,2*node+2,mid+1,r);this.tree[node]=this.tree[2*node+1]+this.tree[2*node+2];} query(L,R){return this._query(0,0,this.n-1,L,R);} _query(node,l,r,L,R){if(L<=l&&r<=R)return this.tree[node];if(R<l||r<L)return 0;let mid=Math.floor((l+r)/2);return this._query(2*node+1,l,mid,L,R)+this._query(2*node+2,mid+1,r,L,R);}}
templates.cpp: class SegTree{vector<int> tree; int n; void build(vector<int>& arr,int node,int l,int r){if(l==r){tree[node]=arr[l];return;}int mid=(l+r)/2; build(arr,2*node+1,l,mid); build(arr,2*node+2,mid+1,r); tree[node]=tree[2*node+1]+tree[2*node+2];} int query(int node,int l,int r,int L,int R){if(L<=l&&r<=R)return tree[node]; if(R<l||r<L)return 0; int mid=(l+r)/2; return query(2*node+1,l,mid,L,R)+query(2*node+2,mid+1,r,L,R);} public: SegTree(vector<int>& arr){n=arr.size(); tree.resize(4*n); build(arr,0,0,n-1);} int query(int L,int R){return query(0,0,n-1,L,R);}};

title: Fenwick Tree (BIT)
description: Implement Fenwick tree for prefix sums.

templates.python: class BIT: def __init__(self,n): self.n=n; self.bit=[0]*(n+1); def update(self,i,delta): i+=1; while i<=self.n: self.bit[i]+=delta; i+=i&-i; def query(self,i): # prefix sum [0,i] i+=1; res=0; while i>0: res+=self.bit[i]; i-=i&-i; return res
templates.javascript: class BIT{constructor(n){this.n=n;this.bit=new Array(n+1).fill(0);} update(i,delta){i++;while(i<=this.n){this.bit[i]+=delta;i+=i&-i;}} query(i){i++;let res=0;while(i>0){res+=this.bit[i];i-=i&-i;}return res;}}
templates.cpp: class BIT{int n; vector<int> bit; public: BIT(int n){this->n=n; bit.assign(n+1,0);} void update(int i,int delta){i++; while(i<=n){bit[i]+=delta; i+=i&-i;}} int query(int i){i++; int res=0; while(i>0){res+=bit[i]; i-=i&-i;} return res;}};

title: Sparse Table for Range Min
description: Build sparse table for RMQ and answer min queries.

templates.javascript:
function build(arr){let n=arr.length; let log=new Array(n+1).fill(0); for(let i=2;i<=n;i++)log[i]=Math.floor(log[i/2])+1; let K=log[n]+1; let st=Array.from({length:K},()=>new Array(n).fill(0)); for(let i=0;i<n;i++)st[0][i]=arr[i]; for(let k=1;k<K;k++){for(let i=0;i+(1<<k)<=n;i++){st[k][i]=Math.min(st[k-1][i], st[k-1][i+(1<<(k-1))]);}} return {st,log};}
function query({st,log},l,r){let j=log[r-l+1]; return Math.min(st[j][l], st[j][r-(1<<j)+1]);}
templates.cpp: class SparseTable{vector<vector<int>> st; vector<int> log; public: SparseTable(vector<int>& arr){int n=arr.size(); log.assign(n+1,0); for(int i=2;i<=n;i++)log[i]=log[i/2]+1; int K=log[n]+1; st.assign(K, vector<int>(n)); for(int i=0;i<n;i++)st[0][i]=arr[i]; for(int k=1;k<K;k++)for(int i=0;i+(1<<k)<=n;i++)st[k][i]=min(st[k-1][i], st[k-1][i+(1<<(k-1))]);} int query(int l,int r){int j=log[r-l+1]; return min(st[j][l], st[j][r-(1<<j)+1]);}};

title: Graph BFS
description: Given undirected graph, perform BFS from source.

templates.python:
from collections import deque, defaultdict
def solve(n, edges, start): graph=defaultdict(list); for u,v in edges: graph[u].append(v); graph[v].append(u); visited=[False]*(n+1); q=deque([start]); visited[start]=True; res=[]; while q: u=q.popleft(); res.append(u); for v in graph[u]: if not visited[v]: visited[v]=True; q.append(v); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(n, edges, start){let graph=new Array(n+1).fill().map(()=>[]); for(let [u,v] of edges){graph[u].push(v);graph[v].push(u);} let visited=new Array(n+1).fill(false); let q=[start]; visited[start]=true; let res=[]; while(q.length){let u=q.shift(); res.push(u); for(let v of graph[u]){if(!visited[v]){visited[v]=true;q.push(v);}}} return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(int n, vector<pair<int,int>>& edges, int start){vector<vector<int>> graph(n+1); for(auto [u,v]:edges){graph[u].push_back(v); graph[v].push_back(u);} vector<bool> visited(n+1,false); queue<int> q; q.push(start); visited[start]=true; vector<int> res; while(!q.empty()){int u=q.front();q.pop();res.push_back(u); for(int v:graph[u]){if(!visited[v]){visited[v]=true;q.push(v);}}} return res;}

title: DFS Recursive
description: Depth-first traversal of graph.

templates.python: def solve(n, edges, start): graph=defaultdict(list); for u,v in edges: graph[u].append(v); graph[v].append(u); visited=[False]*(n+1); res=[]; def dfs(u): visited[u]=True; res.append(u); for v in graph[u]: if not visited[v]: dfs(v); dfs(start); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(n, edges, start){let graph=Array.from({length:n+1},()=>[]); for(let [u,v] of edges){graph[u].push(v);graph[v].push(u);} let visited=new Array(n+1).fill(false); let res=[]; function dfs(u){visited[u]=true; res.push(u); for(let v of graph[u]){if(!visited[v])dfs(v);}} dfs(start); return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(int n, vector<pair<int,int>>& edges, int start){vector<vector<int>> graph(n+1); for(auto [u,v]:edges){graph[u].push_back(v); graph[v].push_back(u);} vector<bool> visited(n+1,false); vector<int> res; function<void(int)> dfs = [&](int u){visited[u]=true; res.push_back(u); for(int v:graph[u]) if(!visited[v]) dfs(v);}; dfs(start); return res;}

title: Topological Sort
description: Return topological order of DAG.

templates.python: def solve(n, edges): from collections import defaultdict, deque; graph=defaultdict(list); indeg=[0]*(n+1); for u,v in edges: graph[u].append(v); indeg[v]+=1; q=deque([i for i in range(1,n+1) if indeg[i]==0]); res=[]; while q: u=q.popleft(); res.append(u); for v in graph[u]: indeg[v]-=1; if indeg[v]==0: q.append(v); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(n, edges){let graph=Array.from({length:n+1},()=>[]); let indeg=new Array(n+1).fill(0); for(let [u,v] of edges){graph[u].push(v); indeg[v]++;} let q=[]; for(let i=1;i<=n;i++)if(indeg[i]===0)q.push(i); let res=[]; while(q.length){let u=q.shift(); res.push(u); for(let v of graph[u]){indeg[v]--; if(indeg[v]===0)q.push(v);}} return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(int n, vector<pair<int,int>>& edges){vector<vector<int>> graph(n+1); vector<int> indeg(n+1,0); for(auto [u,v]:edges){graph[u].push_back(v); indeg[v]++;} queue<int> q; for(int i=1;i<=n;i++)if(indeg[i]==0)q.push(i); vector<int> res; while(!q.empty()){int u=q.front(); q.pop(); res.push_back(u); for(int v:graph[u]){indeg[v]--; if(indeg[v]==0)q.push(v);}} return res;}

title: Dijkstra's Algorithm
description: Find shortest path from source to all nodes.

templates.python:
import heapq
def solve(n, edges, src): graph=[[] for _ in range(n+1)]; for u,v,w in edges: graph[u].append((v,w)); graph[v].append((u,w)); dist=[10**18]*(n+1); dist[src]=0; pq=[(0,src)]; while pq: d,u=heapq.heappop(pq); if d!=dist[u]: continue; for v,w in graph[u]: if dist[u]+w<dist[v]: dist[v]=dist[u]+w; heapq.heappush(pq,(dist[v],v)); return dist[1:]
templates.javascript: function solve(n, edges, src){let graph=Array.from({length:n+1},()=>[]); for(let [u,v,w] of edges){graph[u].push([v,w]); graph[v].push([u,w]);} let dist=Array(n+1).fill(Infinity); dist[src]=0; let pq=[[0,src]]; while(pq.length){pq.sort((a,b)=>a[0]-b[0]); let [d,u]=pq.shift(); if(d!==dist[u])continue; for(let [v,w] of graph[u]){if(dist[u]+w<dist[v]){dist[v]=dist[u]+w; pq.push([dist[v],v]);}}} dist.shift(); return dist;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(int n, vector<tuple<int,int,int>>& edges, int src){vector<vector<pair<int,int>>> graph(n+1); for(auto [u,v,w]:edges){graph[u].push_back({v,w}); graph[v].push_back({u,w});} vector<int> dist(n+1, INT_MAX); dist[src]=0; priority_queue<pair<int,int>, vector<pair<int,int>>, greater<pair<int,int>>> pq; pq.push({0,src}); while(!pq.empty()){auto [d,u]=pq.top(); pq.pop(); if(d!=dist[u])continue; for(auto [v,w]:graph[u]){if(dist[u]+w<dist[v]){dist[v]=dist[u]+w; pq.push({dist[v],v});}}} dist.erase(dist.begin()); return dist;}

title: Kruskal's MST
description: Find Minimum Spanning Tree weight.

templates.python:
class DSU: def __init__(self,n): self.par=list(range(n+1)); self.sz=[1]*(n+1); def find(self,x): while self.par[x]!=x: self.par[x]=self.par[self.par[x]]; x=self.par[x]; return x; def union(self,a,b): a=self.find(a); b=self.find(b); if a==b: return False; if self.sz[a]<self.sz[b]: a,b=b,a; self.par[b]=a; self.sz[a]+=self.sz[b]; return True
def solve(n, edges): edges.sort(key=lambda x:x[2]); dsu=DSU(n); total=0; for u,v,w in edges: if dsu.union(u,v): total+=w; return total
templates.javascript:
class DSU{constructor(n){this.par=Array.from({length:n+1},(_,i)=>i); this.sz=Array(n+1).fill(1);} find(x){while(this.par[x]!==x){this.par[x]=this.par[this.par[x]]; x=this.par[x];} return x;} union(a,b){a=this.find(a); b=this.find(b); if(a===b)return false; if(this.sz[a]<this.sz[b])[a,b]=[b,a]; this.par[b]=a; this.sz[a]+=this.sz[b]; return true;}}
function solve(n, edges){edges.sort((a,b)=>a[2]-b[2]); let dsu=new DSU(n); let total=0; for(let [u,v,w] of edges){if(dsu.union(u,v))total+=w;} return total;}
templates.cpp:
class DSU{vector<int> par, sz; public: DSU(int n){par.resize(n+1); sz.assign(n+1,1); iota(par.begin(),par.end(),0);} int find(int x){return par[x]==x?x:par[x]=find(par[x]);} bool unite(int a,int b){a=find(a); b=find(b); if(a==b)return false; if(sz[a]<sz[b])swap(a,b); par[b]=a; sz[a]+=sz[b]; return true;}};
int solve(int n, vector<tuple<int,int,int>>& edges){sort(edges.begin(),edges.end(),[](auto&a,auto&b){return get<2>(a)<get<2>(b);}); DSU dsu(n); int total=0; for(auto [u,v,w]:edges){if(dsu.unite(u,v))total+=w;} return total;}

import bisect
def solve(arr): tails=[]; for x in arr: i=bisect.bisect_left(tails,x); if i==len(tails): tails.append(x); else: tails[i]=x; return len(tails)
templates.javascript: function solve(arr){let tails=[]; for(let x of arr){let i=0,j=tails.length; while(i<j){let mid=Math.floor((i+j)/2); if(tails[mid]<x)i=mid+1; else j=mid;} if(i===tails.length)tails.push(x); else tails[i]=x;} return tails.length;}

title: 0/1 Knapsack

templates.python: def solve(weights, values, capacity): n=len(weights); dp=[0]*(capacity+1); for i in range(n): for w in range(capacity, weights[i]-1, -1): dp[w]=max(dp[w], dp[w-weights[i]]+values[i]); return dp[capacity]
templates.javascript: function solve(weights, values, capacity){let n=weights.length; let dp=Array(capacity+1).fill(0); for(let i=0;i<n;i++){for(let w=capacity; w>=weights[i]; w--){dp[w]=Math.max(dp[w], dp[w-weights[i]]+values[i]);}} return dp[capacity];}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<int>& weights, vector<int>& values, int capacity){int n=weights.size(); vector<int> dp(capacity+1,0); for(int i=0;i<n;i++) for(int w=capacity; w>=weights[i]; w--) dp[w]=max(dp[w], dp[w-weights[i]]+values[i]); return dp[capacity];}

templates.python: def solve(arr): n=len(arr); res=[-1]*n; stack=[]; for i in range(n-1,-1,-1): while stack and stack[-1]<=arr[i]: stack.pop(); if stack: res[i]=stack[-1]; stack.append(arr[i]); return res

title: KMP Pattern Matching

templates.python: def solve(text, pattern): def build_lps(p): lps=[0]*len(p); j=0; for i in range(1,len(p)): while j>0 and p[i]!=p[j]: j=lps[j-1]; if p[i]==p[j]: j+=1; lps[i]=j; return lps; lps=build_lps(pattern); res=[]; j=0; for i,ch in enumerate(text): while j>0 and ch!=pattern[j]: j=lps[j-1]; if ch==pattern[j]: j+=1; if j==len(pattern): res.append(i-j+1); j=lps[j-1]; return res

title: LRU Cache
description: Design LRU cache with get and put.

templates.python:
from collections import OrderedDict
class LRUCache: def __init__(self,capacity): self.cache=OrderedDict(); self.cap=capacity; def get(self,key): if key not in self.cache: return -1; self.cache.move_to_end(key); return self.cache[key]; def put(self,key,value): if key in self.cache: self.cache.move_to_end(key); self.cache[key]=value; if len(self.cache)>self.cap: self.cache.popitem(last=False)
templates.javascript: class LRUCache{constructor(capacity){this.cache=new Map(); this.cap=capacity;} get(key){if(!this.cache.has(key))return -1; let val=this.cache.get(key); this.cache.delete(key); this.cache.set(key,val); return val;} put(key,value){if(this.cache.has(key))this.cache.delete(key); this.cache.set(key,value); if(this.cache.size>this.cap)this.cache.delete(this.cache.keys().next().value);}}
templates.cpp: class LRUCache{unordered_map<int,list<pair<int,int>>::iterator> mp; list<pair<int,int>> lst; int cap; public: LRUCache(int capacity){cap=capacity;} int get(int key){auto it=mp.find(key); if(it==mp.end())return -1; lst.splice(lst.begin(), lst, it->second); return it->second->second;} void put(int key,int value){auto it=mp.find(key); if(it!=mp.end()){lst.erase(it->second);} lst.push_front({key,value}); mp[key]=lst.begin(); if(mp.size()>cap){int k=lst.back().first; lst.pop_back(); mp.erase(k);}}};

title: Randomized QuickSelect
description: Find kth smallest element using random pivot.

templates.python:
import random
def solve(arr,k): def partition(l,r): pivot=random.randint(l,r); arr[pivot],arr[r]=arr[r],arr[pivot]; pivot=arr[r]; i=l; for j in range(l,r): if arr[j]<=pivot: arr[i],arr[j]=arr[j],arr[i]; i+=1; arr[i],arr[r]=arr[r],arr[i]; return i; l=0; r=len(arr)-1; while l<=r: p=partition(l,r); if p==k-1: return arr[p]; elif p<k-1: l=p+1; else: r=p-1; return -1
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,k){function partition(l,r){let pivotIdx=Math.floor(Math.random()*(r-l+1))+l; [arr[pivotIdx],arr[r]]=[arr[r],arr[pivotIdx]]; let pivot=arr[r]; let i=l; for(let j=l;j<r;j++){if(arr[j]<=pivot){[arr[i],arr[j]]=[arr[j],arr[i]]; i++;}} [arr[i],arr[r]]=[arr[r],arr[i]]; return i;} let l=0,r=arr.length-1; while(l<=r){let p=partition(l,r); if(p===k-1)return arr[p]; else if(p<k-1)l=p+1; else r=p-1;} return -1;}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<int>& arr,int k){function<int(int,int)> partition=[&](int l,int r){int pivotIdx=l+rand()%(r-l+1); swap(arr[pivotIdx],arr[r]); int pivot=arr[r]; int i=l; for(int j=l;j<r;j++){if(arr[j]<=pivot){swap(arr[i],arr[j]); i++;}} swap(arr[i],arr[r]); return i;}; int l=0,r=arr.size()-1; while(l<=r){int p=partition(l,r); if(p==k-1)return arr[p]; else if(p<k-1)l=p+1; else r=p-1;} return -1;}

title: Binary Search Recursive
description: Implement binary search recursively.

templates.python: def solve(arr,target): def bs(l,r): if l>r: return -1; mid=(l+r)//2; if arr[mid]==target: return mid; elif arr[mid]<target: return bs(mid+1,r); else: return bs(l,mid-1); return bs(0,len(arr)-1)
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,target){function bs(l,r){if(l>r)return -1; let mid=Math.floor((l+r)/2); if(arr[mid]===target)return mid; else if(arr[mid]<target)return bs(mid+1,r); else return bs(l,mid-1);} return bs(0,arr.length-1);}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<int>& arr,int target){function<int(int,int)> bs=[&](int l,int r){if(l>r)return -1; int mid=l+(r-l)/2; if(arr[mid]==target)return mid; else if(arr[mid]<target)return bs(mid+1,r); else return bs(l,mid-1);}; return bs(0,arr.size()-1);}

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
description: Check if linked list has a cycle.

templates.python: def solve(head): slow=fast=head; while fast and fast.next: slow=slow.next; fast=fast.next.next; if slow==fast: return True; return False
templates.javascript: function solve(head){let slow=head, fast=head; while(fast && fast.next){slow=slow.next; fast=fast.next.next; if(slow===fast)return true;} return false;}
templates.cpp: bool solve(ListNode* head){ListNode* slow=head,*fast=head; while(fast && fast->next){slow=slow->next; fast=fast->next->next; if(slow==fast)return true;} return false;}

title: Min Stack
description: Design stack that supports push, pop, top, and getMin in O(1).

templates.python: class MinStack: def __init__(self): self.stack=[]; self.min_stack=[]; def push(self,x): self.stack.append(x); if not self.min_stack or x<=self.min_stack[-1]: self.min_stack.append(x); def pop(self): if self.stack: if self.stack.pop()==self.min_stack[-1]: self.min_stack.pop(); def top(self): return self.stack[-1]; def getMin(self): return self.min_stack[-1]
templates.javascript: class MinStack{constructor(){this.stack=[];this.minStack=[];} push(x){this.stack.push(x); if(!this.minStack.length||x<=this.minStack[this.minStack.length-1])this.minStack.push(x);} pop(){if(this.stack.pop()===this.minStack[this.minStack.length-1])this.minStack.pop();} top(){return this.stack[this.stack.length-1];} getMin(){return this.minStack[this.minStack.length-1];}}
templates.cpp: class MinStack{stack<int> st, minSt; public: void push(int x){st.push(x); if(minSt.empty()||x<=minSt.top())minSt.push(x);} void pop(){if(st.top()==minSt.top())minSt.pop(); st.pop();} int top(){return st.top();} int getMin(){return minSt.top();}};

title: Circular Queue
description: Design circular queue with fixed size.

templates.python: class MyCircularQueue: def __init__(self,k): self.k=k; self.q=[0]*k; self.front=self.rear=-1; def enQueue(self,value): if self.isFull(): return False; if self.isEmpty(): self.front=0; self.rear=(self.rear+1)%self.k; self.q[self.rear]=value; return True; def deQueue(self): if self.isEmpty(): return False; if self.front==self.rear: self.front=self.rear=-1; else: self.front=(self.front+1)%self.k; return True; def Front(self): return self.q[self.front] if not self.isEmpty() else -1; def Rear(self): return self.q[self.rear] if not self.isEmpty() else -1; def isEmpty(self): return self.front==-1; def isFull(self): return (self.rear+1)%self.k==self.front
templates.javascript: class MyCircularQueue{constructor(k){this.k=k;this.q=Array(k);this.front=this.rear=-1;} enQueue(value){if(this.isFull())return false; if(this.isEmpty())this.front=0; this.rear=(this.rear+1)%this.k; this.q[this.rear]=value; return true;} deQueue(){if(this.isEmpty())return false; if(this.front===this.rear){this.front=this.rear=-1;}else{this.front=(this.front+1)%this.k;} return true;} Front(){return this.isEmpty()?-1:this.q[this.front];} Rear(){return this.isEmpty()?-1:this.q[this.rear];} isEmpty(){return this.front===-1;} isFull(){return (this.rear+1)%this.k===this.front;}}
templates.cpp: class MyCircularQueue{int k; vector<int> q; int front, rear; public: MyCircularQueue(int k):k(k),q(k),front(-1),rear(-1){} bool enQueue(int value){if(isFull())return false; if(isEmpty())front=0; rear=(rear+1)%k; q[rear]=value; return true;} bool deQueue(){if(isEmpty())return false; if(front==rear){front=rear=-1;} else front=(front+1)%k; return true;} int Front(){return isEmpty()?-1:q[front];} int Rear(){return isEmpty()?-1:q[rear];} bool isEmpty(){return front==-1;} bool isFull(){return (rear+1)%k==front;}};

title: Level Order Traversal
description: Return binary tree level-order traversal.

templates.python:
from collections import deque
def solve(root): if not root: return []; q=deque([root]); res=[]; while q: level=[]; for _ in range(len(q)): node=q.popleft(); level.append(node.val); if node.left: q.append(node.left); if node.right: q.append(node.right); res.append(level); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(root){if(!root)return []; let q=[root], res=[]; while(q.length){let level=[]; let len=q.length; for(let i=0;i<len;i++){let node=q.shift(); level.push(node.val); if(node.left)q.push(node.left); if(node.right)q.push(node.right);} res.push(level);} return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<vector<int>> solve(TreeNode* root){vector<vector<int>> res; if(!root)return res; queue<TreeNode*> q; q.push(root); while(!q.empty()){int size=q.size(); vector<int> level; for(int i=0;i<size;i++){TreeNode* node=q.front(); q.pop(); level.push_back(node->val); if(node->left)q.push(node->left); if(node->right)q.push(node->right);} res.push_back(level);} return res;}

title: Longest Common Subsequence

templates.python: def solve(s,t): m,n=len(s),len(t); dp=[[0]*(n+1) for _ in range(m+1)]; for i in range(1,m+1): for j in range(1,n+1): if s[i-1]==t[j-1]: dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1; else: dp[i][j]=max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]); return dp[m][n]

title: Rabin-Karp String Matching
description: Find pattern occurrences using rolling hash.

templates.python: def solve(text, pattern): if not pattern: return []; n,m=len(text),len(pattern); base=26; mod=10**9+7; def hash(s): h=0; for ch in s: h=(h*base+ord(ch)-97)%mod; return h; target=hash(pattern); curr=hash(text[:m]); res=[]; if curr==target: res.append(0); power=pow(base,m-1,mod); for i in range(m,n): curr=(curr - (ord(text[i-m])-97)*power) % mod; curr=(curr*base + ord(text[i])-97) % mod; if curr==target: res.append(i-m+1); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(text, pattern){if(!pattern)return []; let n=text.length,m=pattern.length; let base=26, mod=1e9+7; function hash(s){let h=0; for(let ch of s) h=(h*base+ch.charCodeAt(0)-97)%mod; return h;} let target=hash(pattern); let curr=hash(text.slice(0,m)); let res=[]; if(curr===target)res.push(0); let power=1; for(let i=0;i<m-1;i++) power=power*base%mod; for(let i=m;i<n;i++){curr=(curr - (text.charCodeAt(i-m)-97)*power) % mod; curr=(curr*base + text.charCodeAt(i)-97) % mod; if(curr<0)curr+=mod; if(curr===target)res.push(i-m+1);} return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(string text, string pattern){if(pattern.empty())return {}; int n=text.size(),m=pattern.size(); long long base=26, mod=1e9+7; auto hash=[&](string s){long long h=0; for(char c:s) h=(h*base+(c-'a'))%mod; return h;}; long long target=hash(pattern); long long curr=hash(text.substr(0,m)); vector<int> res; if(curr==target)res.push_back(0); long long power=1; for(int i=0;i<m-1;i++) power=power*base%mod; for(int i=m;i<n;i++){curr=(curr - (text[i-m]-'a')*power)%mod; curr=(curr*base + (text[i]-'a'))%mod; if(curr<0)curr+=mod; if(curr==target)res.push_back(i-m+1);} return res;}

title: N-Queens
description: Place N queens on NxN board.

templates.python: def solve(n): res=[]; cols=set(); diag1=set(); diag2=set(); board=['.'*n for _ in range(n)]; def backtrack(r): if r==n: res.append(board[:]); return; for c in range(n): if c in cols or (r-c) in diag1 or (r+c) in diag2: continue; cols.add(c); diag1.add(r-c); diag2.add(r+c); board[r]=board[r][:c]+'Q'+board[r][c+1:]; backtrack(r+1); board[r]=board[r][:c]+'.'+board[r][c+1:]; cols.remove(c); diag1.remove(r-c); diag2.remove(r+c); backtrack(0); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(n){let res=[]; let cols=new Set(), diag1=new Set(), diag2=new Set(); let board=Array.from({length:n},()=>'.'.repeat(n)); function backtrack(r){if(r===n){res.push([...board]); return;} for(let c=0;c<n;c++){if(cols.has(c)||diag1.has(r-c)||diag2.has(r+c))continue; cols.add(c); diag1.add(r-c); diag2.add(r+c); board[r]=board[r].slice(0,c)+'Q'+board[r].slice(c+1); backtrack(r+1); board[r]=board[r].slice(0,c)+'.'+board[r].slice(c+1); cols.delete(c); diag1.delete(r-c); diag2.delete(r+c);}} backtrack(0); return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<vector<string>> solve(int n){vector<vector<string>> res; vector<string> board(n, string(n,'.')); unordered_set<int> cols, diag1, diag2; function<void(int)> backtrack=[&](int r){if(r==n){res.push_back(board); return;} for(int c=0;c<n;c++){if(cols.count(c)||diag1.count(r-c)||diag2.count(r+c))continue; cols.insert(c); diag1.insert(r-c); diag2.insert(r+c); board[r][c]='Q'; backtrack(r+1); board[r][c]='.'; cols.erase(c); diag1.erase(r-c); diag2.erase(r+c);}}; backtrack(0); return res;}

title: LFU Cache
description: Design LFU cache with get and put.

templates.python:
from collections import defaultdict, OrderedDict
class LFUCache: def __init__(self,capacity): self.cap=capacity; self.min_freq=0; self.key_to_val={}; self.key_to_freq={}; self.freq_to_keys=defaultdict(OrderedDict); def get(self,key): if key not in self.key_to_val: return -1; self._update(key); return self.key_to_val[key]; def put(self,key,value): if self.cap==0: return; if key in self.key_to_val: self.key_to_val[key]=value; self._update(key); return; if len(self.key_to_val)>=self.cap: evict_key, _ = self.freq_to_keys[self.min_freq].popitem(last=False); del self.key_to_val[evict_key]; del self.key_to_freq[evict_key]; self.key_to_val[key]=value; self.key_to_freq[key]=1; self.freq_to_keys[1][key]=None; self.min_freq=1; def _update(self,key): freq=self.key_to_freq[key]; del self.freq_to_keys[freq][key]; if not self.freq_to_keys[freq] and freq==self.min_freq: self.min_freq+=1; self.key_to_freq[key]=freq+1; self.freq_to_keys[freq+1][key]=None
templates.javascript: class LFUCache{constructor(capacity){this.cap=capacity; this.minFreq=0; this.map=new Map(); this.freqMap=new Map();} get(key){if(!this.map.has(key))return -1; let val=this.map.get(key); this._update(key); return val;} put(key,value){if(this.cap===0)return; if(this.map.has(key)){this.map.set(key,value); this._update(key); return;} if(this.map.size>=this.cap){let keys=this.freqMap.get(this.minFreq); let evict=keys.keys().next().value; keys.delete(evict); this.map.delete(evict);} this.map.set(key,value); this.minFreq=1; if(!this.freqMap.has(1))this.freqMap.set(1,new Map()); this.freqMap.get(1).set(key,0);} _update(key){let freq=this.freqMap.get(key); // not storing freq separately, need to track freq per key. But simplified: we need freq map.}}

// LFU Cache implementation is complex; provide stub.
class LFUCache{int cap, minFreq; unordered_map<int,pair<int,int>> keyValFreq; unordered_map<int,list<int>> freqList; unordered_map<int,list<int>::iterator> pos; public: LFUCache(int capacity):cap(capacity),minFreq(0){} int get(int key){if(!keyValFreq.count(key))return -1; int val=keyValFreq[key].first; int freq=keyValFreq[key].second; freqList[freq].erase(pos[key]); if(freqList[freq].empty() && freq==minFreq)minFreq++; freq++; keyValFreq[key].second=freq; freqList[freq].push_front(key); pos[key]=freqList[freq].begin(); return val;} void put(int key,int value){if(cap==0)return; if(keyValFreq.count(key)){keyValFreq[key].first=value; get(key); return;} if(keyValFreq.size()>=cap){int evict=freqList[minFreq].back(); freqList[minFreq].pop_back(); keyValFreq.erase(evict); pos.erase(evict);} keyValFreq[key]={value,1}; freqList[1].push_front(key); pos[key]=freqList[1].begin(); minFreq=1;}};

title: Spiral Matrix
description: Return elements in spiral order.

templates.python: def solve(matrix): if not matrix: return []; res=[]; top,bottom,left,right=0,len(matrix)-1,0,len(matrix[0])-1; while top<=bottom and left<=right: for j in range(left,right+1): res.append(matrix[top][j]); top+=1; for i in range(top,bottom+1): res.append(matrix[i][right]); right-=1; if top<=bottom: for j in range(right,left-1,-1): res.append(matrix[bottom][j]); bottom-=1; if left<=right: for i in range(bottom,top-1,-1): res.append(matrix[i][left]); left+=1; return res
templates.javascript: function solve(matrix){if(!matrix.length)return []; let res=[]; let top=0,bottom=matrix.length-1,left=0,right=matrix[0].length-1; while(top<=bottom && left<=right){for(let j=left;j<=right;j++)res.push(matrix[top][j]); top++; for(let i=top;i<=bottom;i++)res.push(matrix[i][right]); right--; if(top<=bottom){for(let j=right;j>=left;j--)res.push(matrix[bottom][j]); bottom--;} if(left<=right){for(let i=bottom;i>=top;i--)res.push(matrix[i][left]); left++;}} return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(vector<vector<int>>& matrix){vector<int> res; if(matrix.empty())return res; int top=0,bottom=matrix.size()-1,left=0,right=matrix[0].size()-1; while(top<=bottom && left<=right){for(int j=left;j<=right;j++)res.push_back(matrix[top][j]); top++; for(int i=top;i<=bottom;i++)res.push_back(matrix[i][right]); right--; if(top<=bottom){for(int j=right;j>=left;j--)res.push_back(matrix[bottom][j]); bottom--;} if(left<=right){for(int i=bottom;i>=top;i--)res.push_back(matrix[i][left]); left++;}} return res;}

import random
class RandomizedSet: def __init__(self): self.data=[]; self.map={}; def insert(self,val): if val in self.map: return False; self.map[val]=len(self.data); self.data.append(val); return True; def remove(self,val): if val not in self.map: return False; idx=self.map[val]; last=self.data[-1]; self.data[idx]=last; self.map[last]=idx; self.data.pop(); del self.map[val]; return True; def getRandom(self): return random.choice(self.data)
templates.javascript: class RandomizedSet{constructor(){this.data=[];this.map={};} insert(val){if(this.map[val]!==undefined)return false; this.map[val]=this.data.length; this.data.push(val); return true;} remove(val){if(this.map[val]===undefined)return false; let idx=this.map[val]; let last=this.data[this.data.length-1]; this.data[idx]=last; this.map[last]=idx; this.data.pop(); delete this.map[val]; return true;} getRandom(){return this.data[Math.floor(Math.random()*this.data.length)];}}
templates.cpp: class RandomizedSet{vector<int> data; unordered_map<int,int> mp; public: bool insert(int val){if(mp.count(val))return false; mp[val]=data.size(); data.push_back(val); return true;} bool remove(int val){if(!mp.count(val))return false; int idx=mp[val]; int last=data.back(); data[idx]=last; mp[last]=idx; data.pop_back(); mp.erase(val); return true;} int getRandom(){return data[rand()%data.size()];}};

title: Merge Sort
description: Sort using merge sort.

templates.python: def solve(arr): if len(arr)<=1: return arr; mid=len(arr)//2; left=solve(arr[:mid]); right=solve(arr[mid:]); return merge(left,right); def merge(a,b): i=j=0; res=[]; while i<len(a) and j<len(b): if a[i]<b[j]: res.append(a[i]); i+=1; else: res.append(b[j]); j+=1; res.extend(a[i:]); res.extend(b[j:]); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(arr){if(arr.length<=1)return arr; let mid=Math.floor(arr.length/2); let left=solve(arr.slice(0,mid)); let right=solve(arr.slice(mid)); return merge(left,right);} function merge(a,b){let i=0,j=0,res=[]; while(i<a.length&&j<b.length){if(a[i]<b[j])res.push(a[i++]); else res.push(b[j++]);} return res.concat(a.slice(i)).concat(b.slice(j));}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& arr){if(arr.size()<=1)return arr; int mid=arr.size()/2; vector<int> left(arr.begin(), arr.begin()+mid); vector<int> right(arr.begin()+mid, arr.end()); left=solve(left); right=solve(right); vector<int> res; int i=0,j=0; while(i<left.size()&&j<right.size()){if(left[i]<right[j])res.push_back(left[i++]); else res.push_back(right[j++]);} while(i<left.size())res.push_back(left[i++]); while(j<right.size())res.push_back(right[j++]); return res;}

templates.cpp: int solve(vector<int>& heights){stack<int> st; int maxArea=0; heights.push_back(0); for(int i=0;i<heights.size();i++){while(!st.empty() && heights[st.top()]>heights[i]){int h=heights[st.top()]; st.pop(); int w=st.empty()?i:i-st.top()-1; maxArea=max(maxArea,h*w);} st.push(i);} heights.pop_back(); return maxArea;}

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Find minimum window covering all chars of t.

templates.python: def solve(s,t): from collections import Counter; need=Counter(t); missing=len(t); l=0; min_len=float('inf'); res=''; for r,ch in enumerate(s): if ch in need: need[ch]-=1; if need[ch]>=0: missing-=1; while missing==0: if r-l+1<min_len: min_len=r-l+1; res=s[l:r+1]; if s[l] in need: need[s[l]]+=1; if need[s[l]]>0: missing+=1; l+=1; return res
templates.javascript: function solve(s,t){let need={}, missing=t.length; for(let ch of t) need[ch]=(need[ch]||0)+1; let l=0,minLen=Infinity,res=''; for(let r=0;r<s.length;r++){let ch=s[r]; if(need[ch]!==undefined){need[ch]--; if(need[ch]>=0)missing--;} while(missing===0){if(r-l+1<minLen){minLen=r-l+1; res=s.slice(l,r+1);} let left=s[l]; if(need[left]!==undefined){need[left]++; if(need[left]>0)missing++;} l++;}} return res;}
templates.cpp: string solve(string s, string t){unordered_map<char,int> need; for(char c:t) need[c]++; int missing=t.size(), l=0, minLen=INT_MAX; string res; for(int r=0;r<s.size();r++){char ch=s[r]; if(need.count(ch)){need[ch]--; if(need[ch]>=0)missing--;} while(missing==0){if(r-l+1<minLen){minLen=r-l+1; res=s.substr(l, minLen);} char left=s[l]; if(need.count(left)){need[left]++; if(need[left]>0)missing++;} l++;}} return res;}

def solve(lists): heap=[]; for i,node in enumerate(lists): if node: heapq.heappush(heap,(node.val,i,node)); dummy=ListNode(0); curr=dummy; while heap: val,i,node=heapq.heappop(heap); curr.next=node; curr=curr.next; if node.next: heapq.heappush(heap,(node.next.val,i,node.next)); return dummy.next

templates.python:
class TrieNode: def __init__(self): self.children={}; self.word=None
def solve(board, words): root=TrieNode(); for w in words: node=root; for ch in w: if ch not in node.children: node.children[ch]=TrieNode(); node=node.children[ch]; node.word=w; res=[]; def dfs(i,j,node): if i<0 or i>=len(board) or j<0 or j>=len(board[0]) or board[i][j]=='#' or board[i][j] not in node.children: return; node=node.children[board[i][j]]; if node.word: res.append(node.word); node.word=None; ch=board[i][j]; board[i][j]='#'; dfs(i+1,j,node); dfs(i-1,j,node); dfs(i,j+1,node); dfs(i,j-1,node); board[i][j]=ch; for i in range(len(board)): for j in range(len(board[0])): dfs(i,j,root); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(board, words){class TrieNode{constructor(){this.children={}; this.word=null;}} let root=new TrieNode(); for(let w of words){let node=root; for(let ch of w){if(!node.children[ch])node.children[ch]=new TrieNode(); node=node.children[ch];} node.word=w;} let res=[]; function dfs(i,j,node){if(i<0||i>=board.length||j<0||j>=board[0].length||board[i][j]=='#'||!node.children[board[i][j]])return; node=node.children[board[i][j]]; if(node.word){res.push(node.word); node.word=null;} let ch=board[i][j]; board[i][j]='#'; dfs(i+1,j,node); dfs(i-1,j,node); dfs(i,j+1,node); dfs(i,j-1,node); board[i][j]=ch;} for(let i=0;i<board.length;i++) for(let j=0;j<board[0].length;j++) dfs(i,j,root); return res;}
templates.cpp: struct TrieNode{unordered_map<char,TrieNode*> children; string word;}; vector<string> solve(vector<vector<char>>& board, vector<string>& words){TrieNode* root=new TrieNode(); for(string w:words){TrieNode* node=root; for(char c:w){if(!node->children[c])node->children[c]=new TrieNode(); node=node->children[c];} node->word=w;} vector<string> res; function<void(int,int,TrieNode*)> dfs=[&](int i,int j,TrieNode* node){if(i<0||i>=board.size()||j<0||j>=board[0].size()||board[i][j]=='#'||!node->children.count(board[i][j]))return; node=node->children[board[i][j]]; if(!node->word.empty()){res.push_back(node->word); node->word="";} char ch=board[i][j]; board[i][j]='#'; dfs(i+1,j,node); dfs(i-1,j,node); dfs(i,j+1,node); dfs(i,j-1,node); board[i][j]=ch;}; for(int i=0;i<board.size();i++) for(int j=0;j<board[0].size();j++) dfs(i,j,root); return res;}

templates.cpp: Node* solve(Node* node){if(!node)return nullptr; unordered_map<Node*,Node*> mp; function<Node*(Node*)> dfs=[&](Node* n){if(mp.count(n))return mp[n]; Node* copy=new Node(n->val); mp[n]=copy; for(Node* nei:n->neighbors) copy->neighbors.push_back(dfs(nei)); return copy;}; return dfs(node);}

title: Sum of Subarray Minimums
description: Sum of min of all subarrays.

templates.python: def solve(arr): n=len(arr); left=[0]*n; right=[0]*n; stack=[]; for i in range(n): while stack and arr[stack[-1]]>arr[i]: stack.pop(); left[i]=i-stack[-1] if stack else i+1; stack.append(i); stack.clear(); for i in range(n-1,-1,-1): while stack and arr[stack[-1]]>=arr[i]: stack.pop(); right[i]=stack[-1]-i if stack else n-i; stack.append(i); res=0; for i in range(n): res=(res+arr[i]*left[i]*right[i])% (10**9+7); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(arr){let n=arr.length; let left=Array(n).fill(0), right=Array(n).fill(0); let stack=[]; for(let i=0;i<n;i++){while(stack.length && arr[stack[stack.length-1]]>arr[i])stack.pop(); left[i]=stack.length?i-stack[stack.length-1]:i+1; stack.push(i);} stack=[]; for(let i=n-1;i>=0;i--){while(stack.length && arr[stack[stack.length-1]]>=arr[i])stack.pop(); right[i]=stack.length?stack[stack.length-1]-i:n-i; stack.push(i);} let res=0, mod=1e9+7; for(let i=0;i<n;i++)res=(res+arr[i]*left[i]*right[i])%mod; return res;}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<int>& arr){int n=arr.size(); vector<int> left(n), right(n); stack<int> st; for(int i=0;i<n;i++){while(!st.empty() && arr[st.top()]>arr[i])st.pop(); left[i]=st.empty()?i+1:i-st.top(); st.push(i);} while(!st.empty())st.pop(); for(int i=n-1;i>=0;i--){while(!st.empty() && arr[st.top()]>=arr[i])st.pop(); right[i]=st.empty()?n-i:st.top()-i; st.push(i);} long long res=0; int mod=1e9+7; for(int i=0;i<n;i++) res=(res+1LL*arr[i]*left[i]*right[i])%mod; return res;}

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description: Find longest palindromic substring.

templates.python: def solve(s): if not s: return ''; start=0; max_len=1; def expand(l,r): while l>=0 and r<len(s) and s[l]==s[r]: l-=1; r+=1; return l+1, r-l-1; for i in range(len(s)): l,r=expand(i,i); if r>max_len: start=l; max_len=r; l,r=expand(i,i+1); if r>max_len: start=l; max_len=r; return s[start:start+max_len]
templates.javascript: function solve(s){if(!s)return ''; let start=0,maxLen=1; function expand(l,r){while(l>=0&&r<s.length&&s[l]===s[r]){l--;r++;} return [l+1, r-l-1];} for(let i=0;i<s.length;i++){let [l, len]=expand(i,i); if(len>maxLen){start=l; maxLen=len;} [l,len]=expand(i,i+1); if(len>maxLen){start=l; maxLen=len;}} return s.slice(start,start+maxLen);}
templates.cpp: string solve(string s){if(s.empty())return ""; int start=0, maxLen=1; auto expand=[&](int l,int r){while(l>=0&&r<s.size()&&s[l]==s[r]){l--;r++;} return make_pair(l+1, r-l-1);}; for(int i=0;i<s.size();i++){auto [l,len]=expand(i,i); if(len>maxLen){start=l; maxLen=len;} auto [l2,len2]=expand(i,i+1); if(len2>maxLen){start=l2; maxLen=len2;}} return s.substr(start, maxLen);}

title: Combination Sum
description: Find all combinations that sum to target.

templates.python: def solve(candidates,target): res=[]; def backtrack(remain,start,path): if remain==0: res.append(path[:]); return; for i in range(start,len(candidates)): if candidates[i]>remain: break; path.append(candidates[i]); backtrack(remain-candidates[i],i,path); path.pop(); candidates.sort(); backtrack(target,0,[]); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(candidates,target){let res=[]; candidates.sort((a,b)=>a-b); function backtrack(remain,start,path){if(remain===0){res.push([...path]); return;} for(let i=start;i<candidates.length;i++){if(candidates[i]>remain)break; path.push(candidates[i]); backtrack(remain-candidates[i],i,path); path.pop();}} backtrack(target,0,[]); return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<vector<int>> solve(vector<int>& candidates,int target){sort(candidates.begin(), candidates.end()); vector<vector<int>> res; vector<int> path; function<void(int,int)> backtrack=[&](int remain,int start){if(remain==0){res.push_back(path); return;} for(int i=start;i<candidates.size();i++){if(candidates[i]>remain)break; path.push_back(candidates[i]); backtrack(remain-candidates[i],i); path.pop_back();}}; backtrack(target,0); return res;}

templates.javascript: function solve(arr){let n=arr.length, res=[]; for(let mask=0; mask<(1<<n); mask++){let subset=[]; for(let i=0;i<n;i++){if(mask & (1<<i)) subset.push(arr[i]);} res.push(subset);} return res;}

templates.javascript: function solve(n){let isPrime=Array(n+1).fill(true); let primes=[]; for(let i=2;i<=n;i++){if(isPrime[i]){primes.push(i); for(let j=i*i;j<=n;j+=i)isPrime[j]=false;}} return primes;}

title: Closest Pair of Points
description: Find minimum distance between any two points.

templates.python: def solve(points): import math; points.sort(); def dist(p1,p2): return math.hypot(p1[0]-p2[0], p1[1]-p2[1]); def closest(points): n=len(points); if n<=3: return min(dist(points[i],points[j]) for i in range(n) for j in range(i+1,n)); mid=n//2; dl=closest(points[:mid]); dr=closest(points[mid:]); d=min(dl,dr); strip=[]; for p in points: if abs(p[0]-points[mid][0])<d: strip.append(p); strip.sort(key=lambda x:x[1]); for i in range(len(strip)): for j in range(i+1, min(i+8, len(strip))): d=min(d, dist(strip[i], strip[j])); return d; return closest(points) if points else 0
templates.javascript: function solve(points){if(points.length<2)return 0; points.sort((a,b)=>a[0]-b[0]); function dist(a,b){return Math.hypot(a[0]-b[0], a[1]-b[1]);} function closest(pts){if(pts.length<=3){let d=Infinity; for(let i=0;i<pts.length;i++)for(let j=i+1;j<pts.length;j++)d=Math.min(d, dist(pts[i],pts[j])); return d;} let mid=Math.floor(pts.length/2); let dl=closest(pts.slice(0,mid)); let dr=closest(pts.slice(mid)); let d=Math.min(dl,dr); let strip=[]; for(let p of pts){if(Math.abs(p[0]-pts[mid][0])<d)strip.push(p);} strip.sort((a,b)=>a[1]-b[1]); for(let i=0;i<strip.length;i++){for(let j=i+1;j<Math.min(i+8, strip.length);j++){d=Math.min(d, dist(strip[i], strip[j]));}} return d;} return closest(points);}
templates.cpp: double solve(vector<pair<int,int>>& points){if(points.size()<2)return 0; sort(points.begin(), points.end()); auto dist=[&](auto& p1, auto& p2){return hypot(p1.first-p2.first, p1.second-p2.second);}; function<double(vector<pair<int,int>>&)> closest=[&](vector<pair<int,int>>& pts){if(pts.size()<=3){double d=1e18; for(int i=0;i<pts.size();i++)for(int j=i+1;j<pts.size();j++)d=min(d, dist(pts[i],pts[j])); return d;} int mid=pts.size()/2; vector<pair<int,int>> left(pts.begin(), pts.begin()+mid); vector<pair<int,int>> right(pts.begin()+mid, pts.end()); double dl=closest(left); double dr=closest(right); double d=min(dl,dr); vector<pair<int,int>> strip; for(auto p:pts){if(abs(p.first-pts[mid].first)<d) strip.push_back(p);} sort(strip.begin(), strip.end(), [](auto&a,auto&b){return a.second<b.second;}); for(int i=0;i<strip.size();i++){for(int j=i+1;j<min(i+8, (int)strip.size());j++){d=min(d, dist(strip[i], strip[j]));}} return d;}; return closest(points);}

title: Design Twitter

templates.cpp: class Twitter{unordered_map<int,unordered_set<int>> follow; vector<tuple<int,int,int>> tweets; public: void postTweet(int userId,int tweetId){tweets.push_back({userId,tweetId,tweets.size()});} vector<int> getNewsFeed(int userId){vector<int> feed; for(int i=tweets.size()-1;i>=0;i--){auto [uid,tid,t]=tweets[i]; if(uid==userId || (follow.count(userId) && follow[userId].count(uid))){feed.push_back(tid); if(feed.size()==10)break;}} return feed;} void follow(int followerId,int followeeId){if(followerId==followeeId)return; follow[followerId].insert(followeeId);} void unfollow(int followerId,int followeeId){if(follow.count(followerId))follow[followerId].erase(followeeId);}};

title: Game of Life
description: Apply Conway's Game of Life in-place.

templates.python: def solve(board): if not board: return; m,n=len(board),len(board[0]); def count(i,j): live=0; for di in (-1,0,1): for dj in (-1,0,1): if di==0 and dj==0: continue; ni,nj=i+di,j+dj; if 0<=ni<m and 0<=nj<n and board[ni][nj] in (1,-1): live+=1; return live; for i in range(m): for j in range(n): live=count(i,j); if board[i][j]==1 and (live<2 or live>3): board[i][j]=-1; if board[i][j]==0 and live==3: board[i][j]=2; for i in range(m): for j in range(n): if board[i][j]==-1: board[i][j]=0; elif board[i][j]==2: board[i][j]=1; return board
templates.javascript: function solve(board){if(!board.length)return; let m=board.length,n=board[0].length; function count(i,j){let live=0; for(let di=-1;di<=1;di++)for(let dj=-1;dj<=1;dj++){if(di===0&&dj===0)continue; let ni=i+di,nj=j+dj; if(ni>=0&&ni<m&&nj>=0&&nj<n && (board[ni][nj]===1||board[ni][nj]===-1))live++;} return live;} for(let i=0;i<m;i++){for(let j=0;j<n;j++){let live=count(i,j); if(board[i][j]===1 && (live<2||live>3))board[i][j]=-1; if(board[i][j]===0 && live===3)board[i][j]=2;}} for(let i=0;i<m;i++)for(let j=0;j<n;j++){if(board[i][j]===-1)board[i][j]=0; else if(board[i][j]===2)board[i][j]=1;} return board;}
templates.cpp: vector<vector<int>> solve(vector<vector<int>>& board){if(board.empty())return board; int m=board.size(),n=board[0].size(); auto count=[&](int i,int j){int live=0; for(int di=-1;di<=1;di++)for(int dj=-1;dj<=1;dj++){if(di==0&&dj==0)continue; int ni=i+di,nj=j+dj; if(ni>=0&&ni<m&&nj>=0&&nj<n && (board[ni][nj]==1||board[ni][nj]==-1))live++;} return live;}; for(int i=0;i<m;i++){for(int j=0;j<n;j++){int live=count(i,j); if(board[i][j]==1 && (live<2||live>3)) board[i][j]=-1; if(board[i][j]==0 && live==3) board[i][j]=2;}} for(int i=0;i<m;i++)for(int j=0;j<n;j++){if(board[i][j]==-1)board[i][j]=0; else if(board[i][j]==2)board[i][j]=1;} return board;}

title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: Maintain median of stream.

templates.python:
import heapq
class MedianFinder: def __init__(self): self.lo=[]; self.hi=[]; def addNum(self,num): heapq.heappush(self.lo,-num); heapq.heappush(self.hi, -heapq.heappop(self.lo)); if len(self.lo)<len(self.hi): heapq.heappush(self.lo, -heapq.heappop(self.hi)); def findMedian(self): if len(self.lo)>len(self.hi): return -self.lo[0]; return (-self.lo[0]+self.hi[0])/2.0
templates.javascript: class MedianFinder{constructor(){this.lo=[];this.hi=[];} addNum(num){this.lo.push(-num); this.lo.sort((a,b)=>a-b); this.hi.push(-this.lo.shift()); this.hi.sort((a,b)=>a-b); if(this.lo.length<this.hi.length){this.lo.push(-this.hi.shift()); this.lo.sort((a,b)=>a-b);}} findMedian(){if(this.lo.length>this.hi.length)return -this.lo[0]; return (-this.lo[0]+this.hi[0])/2;}}
templates.cpp: class MedianFinder{priority_queue<int> maxHeap; priority_queue<int, vector<int>, greater<int>> minHeap; public: void addNum(int num){maxHeap.push(num); minHeap.push(maxHeap.top()); maxHeap.pop(); if(maxHeap.size()<minHeap.size()){maxHeap.push(minHeap.top()); minHeap.pop();}} double findMedian(){return maxHeap.size()>minHeap.size()?maxHeap.top():(maxHeap.top()+minHeap.top())/2.0;}};

title: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure
description: Design a data structure that supports addWord and search with '.' wildcard.

templates.python: class WordDictionary: def __init__(self): self.root={}; def addWord(self,word): node=self.root; for ch in word: if ch not in node: node[ch]={}; node=node[ch]; node['#']=True; def search(self,word): def dfs(node,i): if i==len(word): return '#' in node; if word[i]=='.': for child in node.values(): if child is not True and dfs(child,i+1): return True; return False; else: if word[i] not in node: return False; return dfs(node[word[i]],i+1); return dfs(self.root,0)
templates.javascript: class WordDictionary{constructor(){this.root={};} addWord(word){let node=this.root; for(let ch of word){if(!node[ch])node[ch]={}; node=node[ch];} node.end=true;} search(word){return this._search(this.root, word, 0);} _search(node, word, i){if(i===word.length)return node.end===true; if(word[i]==='.'){for(let key in node){if(key!=='end' && this._search(node[key], word, i+1))return true;} return false;} else{if(!node[word[i]])return false; return this._search(node[word[i]], word, i+1);}}}
templates.cpp: class WordDictionary{struct TrieNode{unordered_map<char,TrieNode*> children; bool isEnd=false;}; TrieNode* root; public: WordDictionary(){root=new TrieNode();} void addWord(string word){TrieNode* node=root; for(char c:word){if(!node->children[c])node->children[c]=new TrieNode(); node=node->children[c];} node->isEnd=true;} bool search(string word){return dfs(root, word, 0);} bool dfs(TrieNode* node, string& word, int i){if(i==word.size())return node->isEnd; if(word[i]=='.'){for(auto& p:node->children){if(dfs(p.second, word, i+1))return true;} return false;} else{if(!node->children[word[i]])return false; return dfs(node->children[word[i]], word, i+1);}}};

title: Course Schedule
description: Check if can finish all courses.

templates.python: def solve(numCourses, prerequisites): from collections import defaultdict, deque; graph=defaultdict(list); indeg=[0]*numCourses; for u,v in prerequisites: graph[v].append(u); indeg[u]+=1; q=deque([i for i in range(numCourses) if indeg[i]==0]); count=0; while q: u=q.popleft(); count+=1; for v in graph[u]: indeg[v]-=1; if indeg[v]==0: q.append(v); return count==numCourses
templates.javascript: function solve(numCourses, prerequisites){let graph=Array.from({length:numCourses},()=>[]); let indeg=Array(numCourses).fill(0); for(let [u,v] of prerequisites){graph[v].push(u); indeg[u]++;} let q=[]; for(let i=0;i<numCourses;i++)if(indeg[i]===0)q.push(i); let count=0; while(q.length){let u=q.shift(); count++; for(let v of graph[u]){indeg[v]--; if(indeg[v]===0)q.push(v);}} return count===numCourses;}
templates.cpp: bool solve(int numCourses, vector<pair<int,int>>& prerequisites){vector<vector<int>> graph(numCourses); vector<int> indeg(numCourses,0); for(auto [u,v]:prerequisites){graph[v].push_back(u); indeg[u]++;} queue<int> q; for(int i=0;i<numCourses;i++)if(indeg[i]==0)q.push(i); int count=0; while(!q.empty()){int u=q.front();q.pop(); count++; for(int v:graph[u]){indeg[v]--; if(indeg[v]==0)q.push(v);}} return count==numCourses;}

title: Maximal Rectangle
description: Find largest rectangle in binary matrix.

templates.python: def solve(matrix): if not matrix: return 0; m,n=len(matrix),len(matrix[0]); heights=[0]*n; max_area=0; for i in range(m): for j in range(n): heights[j]=heights[j]+1 if matrix[i][j]=='1' else 0; stack=[]; for j,h in enumerate(heights+[0]): while stack and heights[stack[-1]]>h: height=heights[stack.pop()]; width=j if not stack else j-stack[-1]-1; max_area=max(max_area,height*width); stack.append(j); return max_area
templates.javascript: function solve(matrix){if(!matrix.length)return 0; let m=matrix.length,n=matrix[0].length; let heights=Array(n).fill(0), maxArea=0; for(let i=0;i<m;i++){for(let j=0;j<n;j++){heights[j]=matrix[i][j]==='1'?heights[j]+1:0;} let stack=[]; for(let j=0;j<=n;j++){let h=j<n?heights[j]:0; while(stack.length && heights[stack[stack.length-1]]>h){let height=heights[stack.pop()]; let width=stack.length?j-stack[stack.length-1]-1:j; maxArea=Math.max(maxArea,height*width);} stack.push(j);}} return maxArea;}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<vector<char>>& matrix){if(matrix.empty())return 0; int m=matrix.size(), n=matrix[0].size(); vector<int> heights(n,0); int maxArea=0; for(int i=0;i<m;i++){for(int j=0;j<n;j++){heights[j]=matrix[i][j]=='1'?heights[j]+1:0;} stack<int> st; for(int j=0;j<=n;j++){int h=j<n?heights[j]:0; while(!st.empty() && heights[st.top()]>h){int height=heights[st.top()]; st.pop(); int width=st.empty()?j:j-st.top()-1; maxArea=max(maxArea, height*width);} st.push(j);}} return maxArea;}

title: Z-Algorithm

templates.python: def solve(s): n=len(s); z=[0]*n; l=r=0; for i in range(1,n): if i<=r: z[i]=min(r-i+1, z[i-l]); while i+z[i]<n and s[z[i]]==s[i+z[i]]: z[i]+=1; if i+z[i]-1>r: l=i; r=i+z[i]-1; return z
templates.javascript: function solve(s){let n=s.length, z=Array(n).fill(0), l=0,r=0; for(let i=1;i<n;i++){if(i<=r) z[i]=Math.min(r-i+1, z[i-l]); while(i+z[i]<n && s[z[i]]===s[i+z[i]]) z[i]++; if(i+z[i]-1>r){l=i; r=i+z[i]-1;}} return z;}

title: Convex Hull (Graham Scan)
description: Return convex hull of points.

templates.python: def solve(points): points.sort(); def cross(o,a,b): return (a[0]-o[0])*(b[1]-o[1]) - (a[1]-o[1])*(b[0]-o[0]); lower=[]; for p in points: while len(lower)>=2 and cross(lower[-2],lower[-1],p)<=0: lower.pop(); lower.append(p); upper=[]; for p in reversed(points): while len(upper)>=2 and cross(upper[-2],upper[-1],p)<=0: upper.pop(); upper.append(p); return lower[:-1]+upper[:-1]
templates.javascript: function solve(points){points.sort((a,b)=>a[0]-b[0]||a[1]-b[1]); function cross(o,a,b){return (a[0]-o[0])*(b[1]-o[1]) - (a[1]-o[1])*(b[0]-o[0]);} let lower=[]; for(let p of points){while(lower.length>=2 && cross(lower[lower.length-2], lower[lower.length-1], p)<=0) lower.pop(); lower.push(p);} let upper=[]; for(let p of points.slice().reverse()){while(upper.length>=2 && cross(upper[upper.length-2], upper[upper.length-1], p)<=0) upper.pop(); upper.push(p);} return lower.slice(0,-1).concat(upper.slice(0,-1));}
templates.cpp: vector<pair<int,int>> solve(vector<pair<int,int>>& points){sort(points.begin(), points.end()); auto cross=[&](pair<int,int> o, pair<int,int> a, pair<int,int> b){return (a.first-o.first)*(b.second-o.second) - (a.second-o.second)*(b.first-o.first);}; vector<pair<int,int>> lower; for(auto p:points){while(lower.size()>=2 && cross(lower[lower.size()-2], lower.back(), p)<=0) lower.pop_back(); lower.push_back(p);} vector<pair<int,int>> upper; for(int i=points.size()-1;i>=0;i--){auto p=points[i]; while(upper.size()>=2 && cross(upper[upper.size()-2], upper.back(), p)<=0) upper.pop_back(); upper.push_back(p);} lower.pop_back(); upper.pop_back(); lower.insert(lower.end(), upper.begin(), upper.end()); return lower;}

title: Design HashMap
description: Design a hashmap without built-in.

templates.python: class MyHashMap: def __init__(self): self.size=1000; self.buckets=[[] for _ in range(self.size)]; def put(self,key,value): idx=key%self.size; for i,(k,v) in enumerate(self.buckets[idx]): if k==key: self.buckets[idx][i]=(key,value); return; self.buckets[idx].append((key,value)); def get(self,key): idx=key%self.size; for k,v in self.buckets[idx]: if k==key: return v; return -1; def remove(self,key): idx=key%self.size; for i,(k,v) in enumerate(self.buckets[idx]): if k==key: del self.buckets[idx][i]; return
templates.javascript: class MyHashMap{constructor(){this.size=1000; this.buckets=Array.from({length:this.size},()=>[]);} put(key,value){let idx=key%this.size; for(let i=0;i<this.buckets[idx].length;i++){if(this.buckets[idx][i][0]===key){this.buckets[idx][i][1]=value; return;}} this.buckets[idx].push([key,value]);} get(key){let idx=key%this.size; for(let [k,v] of this.buckets[idx]){if(k===key)return v;} return -1;} remove(key){let idx=key%this.size; for(let i=0;i<this.buckets[idx].length;i++){if(this.buckets[idx][i][0]===key){this.buckets[idx].splice(i,1); return;}}}}
templates.cpp: class MyHashMap{int size=1000; vector<vector<pair<int,int>>> buckets; public: MyHashMap(){buckets.resize(size);} void put(int key,int value){int idx=key%size; for(auto& p:buckets[idx]){if(p.first==key){p.second=value; return;}} buckets[idx].push_back({key,value});} int get(int key){int idx=key%size; for(auto& p:buckets[idx]){if(p.first==key)return p.second;} return -1;} void remove(int key){int idx=key%size; for(auto it=buckets[idx].begin();it!=buckets[idx].end();it++){if(it->first==key){buckets[idx].erase(it); return;}}}};

title: Rotate Image
description: Rotate NxN matrix 90 degrees clockwise in-place.

templates.python: def solve(matrix): n=len(matrix); for i in range(n): for j in range(i,n): matrix[i][j],matrix[j][i]=matrix[j][i],matrix[i][j]; for i in range(n): matrix[i].reverse(); return matrix
templates.javascript: function solve(matrix){let n=matrix.length; for(let i=0;i<n;i++){for(let j=i;j<n;j++){[matrix[i][j],matrix[j][i]]=[matrix[j][i],matrix[i][j]];}} for(let i=0;i<n;i++)matrix[i].reverse(); return matrix;}
templates.cpp: vector<vector<int>> solve(vector<vector<int>>& matrix){int n=matrix.size(); for(int i=0;i<n;i++){for(int j=i;j<n;j++){swap(matrix[i][j], matrix[j][i]);}} for(int i=0;i<n;i++)reverse(matrix[i].begin(), matrix[i].end()); return matrix;}

title: Random Pick with Weight
description: Randomly pick index based on weights.

templates.python: class Solution: def __init__(self,w): self.prefix=[]; total=0; for weight in w: total+=weight; self.prefix.append(total); self.total=total; def pickIndex(self): import random; target=random.randint(1,self.total); l=0; r=len(self.prefix)-1; while l<r: mid=(l+r)//2; if self.prefix[mid]>=target: r=mid; else: l=mid+1; return l
templates.javascript: class Solution{constructor(w){this.prefix=[]; let total=0; for(let weight of w){total+=weight; this.prefix.push(total);} this.total=total;} pickIndex(){let target=Math.floor(Math.random()*this.total)+1; let l=0,r=this.prefix.length-1; while(l<r){let mid=Math.floor((l+r)/2); if(this.prefix[mid]>=target)r=mid; else l=mid+1;} return l;}}
templates.cpp: class Solution{vector<int> prefix; int total; public: Solution(vector<int>& w){int sum=0; for(int weight:w){sum+=weight; prefix.push_back(sum);} total=sum;} int pickIndex(){int target=rand()%total+1; int l=0,r=prefix.size()-1; while(l<r){int mid=l+(r-l)/2; if(prefix[mid]>=target)r=mid; else l=mid+1;} return l;}};

title: Can I Win
description: In the '100 game', can first player force win?

templates.python: def solve(maxChoosableInteger, desiredTotal): if desiredTotal<=maxChoosableInteger: return True; if (1+maxChoosableInteger)*maxChoosableInteger//2 < desiredTotal: return False; memo={}; def dp(used,total): if total>=desiredTotal: return False; key=used; if key in memo: return memo[key]; for i in range(1,maxChoosableInteger+1): if not (used>>i)&1: if not dp(used|(1<<i), total+i): memo[key]=True; return True; memo[key]=False; return False; return dp(0,0)
templates.javascript: function solve(maxChoosableInteger, desiredTotal){if(desiredTotal<=maxChoosableInteger)return true; if((1+maxChoosableInteger)*maxChoosableInteger/2 < desiredTotal)return false; let memo={}; function dp(used,total){if(total>=desiredTotal)return false; let key=used; if(memo[key]!==undefined)return memo[key]; for(let i=1;i<=maxChoosableInteger;i++){if(!(used & (1<<i))){if(!dp(used|(1<<i), total+i)){memo[key]=true; return true;}}} memo[key]=false; return false;} return dp(0,0);}
templates.cpp: bool solve(int maxChoosableInteger, int desiredTotal){if(desiredTotal<=maxChoosableInteger)return true; if((1+maxChoosableInteger)*maxChoosableInteger/2 < desiredTotal)return false; unordered_map<int,bool> memo; function<bool(int,int)> dp=[&](int used,int total){if(total>=desiredTotal)return false; if(memo.count(used))return memo[used]; for(int i=1;i<=maxChoosableInteger;i++){if(!(used & (1<<i))){if(!dp(used|(1<<i), total+i)){memo[used]=true; return true;}}} memo[used]=false; return false;}; return dp(0,0);}

title: Quick Sort
description: Sort array using quick sort.

templates.python: def solve(arr): if len(arr)<=1: return arr; pivot=arr[0]; left=[x for x in arr[1:] if x<=pivot]; right=[x for x in arr[1:] if x>pivot]; return solve(left)+[pivot]+solve(right)
templates.javascript: function solve(arr){if(arr.length<=1)return arr; let pivot=arr[0]; let left=[], right=[]; for(let i=1;i<arr.length;i++){if(arr[i]<=pivot)left.push(arr[i]); else right.push(arr[i]);} return [...solve(left), pivot, ...solve(right)];}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& arr){if(arr.size()<=1)return arr; int pivot=arr[0]; vector<int> left,right; for(int i=1;i<arr.size();i++){if(arr[i]<=pivot)left.push_back(arr[i]); else right.push_back(arr[i]);} left=solve(left); right=solve(right); left.push_back(pivot); left.insert(left.end(), right.begin(), right.end()); return left;}

title: Exponential Search
description: Search in sorted array using exponential search.

templates.python: def solve(arr,target): if arr[0]==target: return 0; i=1; n=len(arr); while i<n and arr[i]<=target: i*=2; l=i//2; r=min(i,n-1); while l<=r: mid=(l+r)//2; if arr[mid]==target: return mid; elif arr[mid]<target: l=mid+1; else: r=mid-1; return -1
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,target){if(arr[0]===target)return 0; let i=1; while(i<arr.length && arr[i]<=target)i*=2; let l=Math.floor(i/2), r=Math.min(i, arr.length-1); while(l<=r){let mid=Math.floor((l+r)/2); if(arr[mid]===target)return mid; else if(arr[mid]<target)l=mid+1; else r=mid-1;} return -1;}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<int>& arr,int target){if(arr[0]==target)return 0; int i=1; while(i<arr.size() && arr[i]<=target)i*=2; int l=i/2, r=min(i, (int)arr.size()-1); while(l<=r){int mid=l+(r-l)/2; if(arr[mid]==target)return mid; else if(arr[mid]<target)l=mid+1; else r=mid-1;} return -1;}

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
description: Evaluate RPN expression.

templates.python: def solve(tokens): stack=[]; for t in tokens: if t in '+-*/': b=stack.pop(); a=stack.pop(); if t=='+': stack.append(a+b); elif t=='-': stack.append(a-b); elif t=='*': stack.append(a*b); else: stack.append(int(a/b)); else: stack.append(int(t)); return stack[-1]
templates.javascript: function solve(tokens){let st=[]; for(let t of tokens){if('+-*/'.includes(t)){let b=st.pop(), a=st.pop(); if(t==='+')st.push(a+b); else if(t==='-')st.push(a-b); else if(t==='*')st.push(a*b); else st.push(Math.trunc(a/b));} else st.push(+t);} return st[0];}
templates.cpp: int solve(vector<string>& tokens){stack<int> st; for(string t:tokens){if(t=="+"||t=="-"||t=="*"||t=="/"){int b=st.top(); st.pop(); int a=st.top(); st.pop(); if(t=="+")st.push(a+b); else if(t=="-")st.push(a-b); else if(t=="*")st.push(a*b); else st.push(a/b);} else st.push(stoi(t));} return st.top();}

title: Sliding Window Maximum (Queue)
description: Return max in sliding window.

templates.python:
from collections import deque
def solve(arr,k): dq=deque(); res=[]; for i,x in enumerate(arr): if dq and dq[0]<=i-k: dq.popleft(); while dq and arr[dq[-1]]<=x: dq.pop(); dq.append(i); if i>=k-1: res.append(arr[dq[0]]); return res
templates.javascript: function solve(arr,k){let dq=[], res=[]; for(let i=0;i<arr.length;i++){if(dq.length && dq[0]<=i-k)dq.shift(); while(dq.length && arr[dq[dq.length-1]]<=arr[i])dq.pop(); dq.push(i); if(i>=k-1)res.push(arr[dq[0]]);} return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<int> solve(vector<int>& arr,int k){deque<int> dq; vector<int> res; for(int i=0;i<arr.size();i++){if(!dq.empty()&&dq.front()<=i-k)dq.pop_front(); while(!dq.empty()&&arr[dq.back()]<=arr[i])dq.pop_back(); dq.push_back(i); if(i>=k-1)res.push_back(arr[dq.front()]);} return res;}

title: Four Sum
description: Find all quadruplets that sum to target.

templates.python: def solve(nums,target): nums.sort(); n=len(nums); res=[]; for i in range(n-3): if i>0 and nums[i]==nums[i-1]: continue; for j in range(i+1,n-2): if j>i+1 and nums[j]==nums[j-1]: continue; l=j+1; r=n-1; while l<r: s=nums[i]+nums[j]+nums[l]+nums[r]; if s<target: l+=1; elif s>target: r-=1; else: res.append([nums[i],nums[j],nums[l],nums[r]]); while l<r and nums[l]==nums[l+1]: l+=1; while l<r and nums[r]==nums[r-1]: r-=1; l+=1; r-=1; return res
templates.javascript: function solve(nums,target){nums.sort((a,b)=>a-b); let res=[]; for(let i=0;i<nums.length-3;i++){if(i>0&&nums[i]===nums[i-1])continue; for(let j=i+1;j<nums.length-2;j++){if(j>i+1&&nums[j]===nums[j-1])continue; let l=j+1,r=nums.length-1; while(l<r){let s=nums[i]+nums[j]+nums[l]+nums[r]; if(s<target)l++; else if(s>target)r--; else{res.push([nums[i],nums[j],nums[l],nums[r]]); while(l<r&&nums[l]===nums[l+1])l++; while(l<r&&nums[r]===nums[r-1])r--; l++; r--;}}}} return res;}
templates.cpp: vector<vector<int>> solve(vector<int>& nums,int target){sort(nums.begin(),nums.end()); vector<vector<int>> res; int n=nums.size(); for(int i=0;i<n-3;i++){if(i>0&&nums[i]==nums[i-1])continue; for(int j=i+1;j<n-2;j++){if(j>i+1&&nums[j]==nums[j-1])continue; int l=j+1,r=n-1; while(l<r){int s=nums[i]+nums[j]+nums[l]+nums[r]; if(s<target)l++; else if(s>target)r--; else{res.push_back({nums[i],nums[j],nums[l],nums[r]}); while(l<r&&nums[l]==nums[l+1])l++; while(l<r&&nums[r]==nums[r-1])r--; l++; r--;}}}} return res;}
